name: Coimbra and Palácio Hotel do Buçaco from Mealhada — circular route
name_original: Coimbra e Palácio Hotel do Buçaco a partir de Mealhada — percurso circular
region_intro: Touring cycling routes around Mealhada traverse a landscape characterized by the dense woodlands of the Mata Nacional do Buçaco and the rolling hills of the Serra do Bussaco. The region features extensive vineyards of the Demarcated Wine Region of Bairrada, offering picturesque and often gentle routes. Elevated points like Alta Cruz provide panoramic views across the terrain. Best touring cycling routes around Mealhada The most popular touring cycling route is Coimbra and Palácio Hotel do Buçaco from Mealhada — circular route, a 61.2 miles (98.4 km) trail that takes 6 hours 35 minutes to complete, passing by the historic Palace Hotel do Buçaco. Another top favourite among local touring cyclists is Bussaco Forest Loop from Anadia, a difficult 26.6 miles (42.8 km) path. This route explores the biodiverse Mata Nacional do Buçaco. Local touring cyclists also love the From Anadia to Barragem da Gralheira — circular route, a 18.3 miles (29.4 km) trail leading through varied countryside towards the Barragem da Gralheira, often completed in about 1 hour 50 minutes. Touring cycling around Mealhada is defined by the ancient Mata Nacional do Buçaco, the elevated Serra do Bussaco, and the expansive Bairrada vineyards. The network offers options for easy, moderate, and difficult ability levels. The routes in Mealhada are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 5.0 stars from over 5 reviews. More than 69 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore Mealhada's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What types of touring cycling routes can I find around Mealhada?
A: Mealhada offers a diverse range of touring cycling routes, from gentle paths through the extensive vineyards of the Demarcated Wine Region of Bairrada to more challenging ascents in the Serra do Bussaco. You'll find routes exploring the ancient Mata Nacional do Buçaco and those offering panoramic views from elevated points like Alta Cruz. The region has options for easy, moderate, and difficult ability levels.

Q: Are there any circular touring cycling routes in the Mealhada area?
A: Yes, Mealhada features several excellent circular touring cycling routes. A popular option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1384690892/coimbra-and-palacio-hotel-do-bucaco-from-mealhada-circular-route" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Coimbra and Palácio Hotel do Buçaco from Mealhada — circular route&lt;/a&gt;, which is a challenging 98.4 km journey. Another great choice is the moderate &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1384694685/from-anadia-to-barragem-da-gralheira-circular-route" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;From Anadia to Barragem da Gralheira — circular route&lt;/a&gt;, covering 29.4 km through varied countryside.

Q: What are the difficulty levels of touring cycling routes in Mealhada?
A: The touring cycling routes around Mealhada cater to various skill levels. Out of approximately 20 routes, you'll find 2 easy, 9 moderate, and 9 difficult options. This variety ensures that both casual riders and experienced touring cyclists can find suitable challenges.

Q: What natural attractions can I explore while cycling in Mealhada?
A: Mealhada is rich in natural beauty. You can cycle through the biodiverse Mata Nacional do Buçaco, a walled garden-arboretum with approximately 400 native and 300 exotic plant species, including a 350-year-old Bussaco cedar. Within the forest, you can discover features like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4840083" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Fonte Fria&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4840088" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Vale das Feteiras&lt;/a&gt;, which offer tranquil environments with lakes, waterfalls, and fountains. The Serra do Bussaco also provides stunning natural vistas.

Q: Are there any historical landmarks or points of interest along the touring cycling routes?
A: Absolutely. Many routes pass by significant historical and architectural sites. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1384690892/coimbra-and-palacio-hotel-do-bucaco-from-mealhada-circular-route" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Coimbra and Palácio Hotel do Buçaco from Mealhada — circular route&lt;/a&gt; takes you past the historic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2009283" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Buçaco Palace Hotel&lt;/a&gt;, a romantic Neo-Manueline palace. The adjacent Convent of Santa Cruz do Buçaco is also a notable stop. Additionally, the region is part of the Camino de Santiago (Portuguese Way), offering a glimpse into ancient pilgrimage paths.

Q: Can I enjoy panoramic views on touring cycling routes in Mealhada?
A: Yes, the Serra do Bussaco offers elevated points with breathtaking panoramic views. The highest point, &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5588829" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cruz Alta Viewpoint&lt;/a&gt;, provides vistas stretching from the Atlantic Ocean to the Caramulo and Estrela Mountains, making it a highly rewarding destination for cyclists seeking elevated scenery.

Q: What do other touring cyclists say about the routes in Mealhada?
A: The touring cycling routes in Mealhada are highly regarded by the komoot community, with an average score of 5.0 stars from over 5 reviews. More than 69 touring cyclists have already used komoot to explore the varied terrain, often praising the scenic beauty of the Mata Nacional do Buçaco and the picturesque Bairrada vineyards.

Q: Is there an entrance fee to cycle through the Mata Nacional do Buçaco?
A: No, entry to the Mata Nacional do Buçaco is free for pedestrians and cyclists. This allows you to freely explore its extensive trails and natural beauty, such as those found on the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1384940897/bussaco-forest-loop-from-anadia" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Bussaco Forest Loop from Anadia&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any routes that pass by vineyards or offer wine-tasting opportunities?
A: Mealhada is located within the Demarcated Wine Region of Bairrada, known for its extensive vineyards. Many routes traverse these picturesque rolling hills, offering a chance to combine your ride with wine-tasting experiences at local wineries. While specific routes aren't solely dedicated to wine tasting, the general landscape provides ample opportunities.

Q: Are there any routes suitable for families or less experienced cyclists?
A: Yes, Mealhada offers routes suitable for various experience levels. While many routes are moderate to difficult, there are easier options available. The gentle terrain through the Bairrada vineyards can be ideal for families or those seeking a more relaxed ride. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/10407333" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Parque de Amoreira da Gândara – Curia Palace Hotel & Gardens loop from Curia&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 34.7 km route that might be suitable for families looking for a slightly longer ride.

Q: What is the best time of year for touring cycling in Mealhada?
A: Mealhada generally offers pleasant conditions for touring cycling for much of the year. Spring and autumn typically provide mild temperatures and beautiful scenery, making them ideal. Summers can be warm, but early mornings or late afternoons are still enjoyable. Winters are generally mild, though some rain is possible.

Q: Are there any routes that offer views of Coimbra?
A: Yes, some routes provide views towards the city of Coimbra. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/3961532" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Parque Verde do Mondego – View of Coimbra loop from Mealhada&lt;/a&gt; is a challenging 74.9 km route specifically designed to offer scenic views, including those of Coimbra.
tips: Segment between Coimbra north and Mealhada south. Expect a great contact with the nature, where you can view a great variety of birds and botanical species. Ride in asphalt, gravel, some rocks and a little bit of sand.

One of the most emblematic and unforgettable panoramas of Coimbra is, without a doubt, the one obtained from the opposite bank of the Mondego River. Another advantage of crossing this area by bike, in addition to the stunning views, is taking advantage of the bike path, which is quite serene and well maintained.
Always stay at this place, very nice view.
Viewpoint in front of a rock formation known as Livraria do Mondego. Just stop here for a few moments to understand why: it resembles books on a shelf. Place of contemplation and peace.

The Aguieira Dam, also known as the Foz do Dão Dam, located on the Mondego River between Penacova and Mortágua, was started in 1973 and inaugurated in 1981. At 89 meters high, it is a multiple arch dam used for hydroelectric production, irrigation , flood control and recreation. Its reservoir has submerged local villages and supplies water to Coimbra and neighboring municipalities.

The Palácio do Buçaco, in Portugal, initially a Carmelite convent from 1628, was transformed into a luxurious hotel in Serra do Buçaco. Today a national monument, it was built between 1888 and 1907 and displays an architectural style inspired by the Portuguese Age of Discoveries.
